Round starts at 6:23. Parker Whitfill negates vs. Oliver Sussman in Round 2 of TOC (they also debate in semis of TOC, where Parker is side locked aff). Judge is Sam Azbel. 2NR goes for evidence ethics, decision is for the negative. Suggestions for redos:

1) Don't miscut or mistag your evidence. Makes winning a lot harder than it needs to be.

2) Redo this 1ar where you generate offense in the 1ar for miscutting ev good OR contest competing interps heavily. Also don't drop that academic honesty comes lexically prior other impacts. 

3) Practice the 2N as is, it's pretty good. Also try the 2N where you just go for substance and kick out of academic honesty.

2017 Tournament of Champions Round 1 PCDS PW (Aff) vs Harvard Westlake EE (Neg)

Parker Whitfill affirms vs. Evan Engel. Decision is for the affirmative, judge is Kris Wright.

Suggestions for redos--
1. Practice a different 1N against Agonism that doesn't rely on key neg generics such as cap K and util + endowments DA. This late into the topic, not having topic specific strategies vs. specific phil affs becomes devastating because 1ARs can extensively practice speeches like Parkers.

2. Practice the 2N and allocate less time (~45 seconds less) on condo and more on the perms. Cut out the word K of paralysis and see how that affects your time allocation.

3. Practice Parker's 2AR where you go for Cap Impact Turns (it's winnable) AND also where you go for Condo Bad (harder debate to win because 2N overallocates on it). Also practice Parker's 2ar as is.

2017 Emory Round 6 PCDS PW (Aff) vs Kinkaid JY (Neg)

Parker Whitfill affirms versus Jason Yang in the 5-0 debate at the 2017 Barkley Forum. 1NC goes for 7 minutes of impact turns to the aff, i.e. heg good. 1AR goes for link turns saying that the aff is the best way to decrease hegemony and that case outweighs. 2NR goes for heg and the 2ar collapses to 2 link turns on the heg debate. Decision is for the affirmative by Akash Gogate. 

This is a phenomenal debate to practice util with--here are some suggestions for redos:
1. Try giving Parker's 1ar and 2ar verbatim. His strategy choices are spot on. Notice how his 1ar puts out several layers (small scale vs big stick impact weighing, case outweighs, link turns) and his 2ar collapses to one those layers (the link turns). 
2. Give Jason's 2NR but a) kick the alternative and b) do more comparative link weighing on who increases heg most. For example, your 2NR overview could be something like "the aff claims they increase heg because of X and Y and the neg claims they increase heg because of A and B, the top level question of this debate is which of these 4 links are the most important. Link A outweighs because of D, E, and F." 
3. Give Parker's 1ar but instead go for heg bad and do 4 minutes of the impact weighing debate.

HW CE affirms against Harrison RP. Panel is Timmons, Zhou, Kukreja. Decision is a 3-0 for the negative. Here are some suggestions for redos:

The topic for this debate was Resolved: Just governments ought to require that employers pay a living wage.

To gain the most out of this video:

1. Flow along--you have to watch the video on its original link via nsdvideos or plug in headphones because audio quality isn't fantastic during the speeches.

2. Take notes on the comments about each speech. Sample notes can be found at debatedrills.com

3. Do the redo drills given at the end of the video, and compare your results vs. my student's and see how you can improve.

Practice Speech 2AR | 2014 Tournament of Champions LD Semifinals

In the video, the debater practices 2AR execution by giving a modified rebuttal based on the 2014 Tournament of Champions Semifinal Round Harrison DD (Aff) vs. Walt Whitman JL (Neg). 

The topic for this debate is Resolved: Developing countries should prioritize environmental protection over resource extraction when the two are in conflict.

To get the most out of this video: 
1. Watch the round and flow- the emphasis should be placed on the 2AR.

2. Watch this video.

3. Prep for the 2AR factoring in the commentary on the theory. 

4. Give a 3-minute 2AR and compare it against the one in this video.

2012 Tournament of Champions LD Semifinals Aliso Niguel RG (neg) vs Loyola BO (aff) Round Analysis

This is an elimination debate (round of 4) at the 2012 Lincoln-Douglas Debate Tournament of Champions between Bob Overing (aff) and Regan Grishaber (Neg). 

The topic for this debate was resolved: It is morally permissible for victims to use deadly force as a deliberate response to repeated domestic violence.

The decision was a 3-0 for the affirmative. This round includes a video analysis of the debate. To gain the most out of this video:
